title: Define test layer responsibility standard
close_reason: Wrote .plan/test-standards.md defining layer responsibilities: VO=unit tests, repo=SQL-specific, service=auth+guards, router=HTTP contract+canonical CRUD.

comments.author: JYC11
comments.text: Document what each test layer should cover: (1) Value objects — pure unit tests, no infra. (2) Repository — SQL-specific only: constraints, JOINs, ltree paths, FK helpers. No CRUD happy paths. (3) Service — business rules only: auth guards, ownership, delete guards, FK validation, cache. (4) Router — HTTP contract: status codes, auth middleware 401/403, pagination, filters, deserialization 422, response shape. Happy-path CRUD lives here as the canonical integration test. Write to .plan/test-standards.md and reference from CLAUDE.md.

title: Implement shared test container (TestDb per binary, not per test)
close_reason: Implemented shared container via OnceCell + CREATE DATABASE TEMPLATE. Identity: 115 tests in 39s. Catalog: 209 tests in 36s. All passing.

comments.author: JYC11
comments.text: Replace per-test TestDb::start() with a shared container per test binary using OnceLock/OnceCell. Each test gets isolation via transaction rollback or TRUNCATE. Collapses ~237 container startups to ~2. Estimated 10-20x speedup. Touches only test infra, zero production code changes. Also consolidate Redis containers in identity (5 → 1).

title: Evolve domain FK references from Option<Uuid> to embedded domain objects

comments.author: JYC11
comments.text:
## Goal

Replace bare `Option<Uuid>` FK references in domain objects with embedded domain types, making the object graph traversable.

## Current State

```rust
// products/domain.rs — FKs are bare UUIDs
pub struct Product {
    pub category_id: Option<Uuid>,  // ← no type safety, no traversal
    pub brand_id: Option<Uuid>,
}
```

## Target State

```rust
// products/domain.rs — FKs are rich domain objects
pub struct Product {
    pub category: Option<Category>,  // ← traversable, type-safe
    pub brand: Option<Brand>,
}
```

## Design Considerations

1. **Loading strategy**: Eager (JOIN at query time) vs lazy (load on access). Current LEFT JOINs already fetch category/brand names — extend to full objects.
2. **Ownership**: Product owns `Option<Brand>` / `Option<Category>` (not `Arc` — these are value-like snapshots, not shared mutable state).
3. **Construction**: `TryFrom<ProductEntity>` would need the joined data to construct embedded domain objects. May need a richer intermediate type or a factory method that takes multiple entities.
4. **Circular refs**: Brand has categories, Product has brand+category. Keep domain objects as DAGs — no cycles.
5. **Partial loading**: Some queries may not need full embedded objects. Consider a `ProductSummary` (IDs only) vs `Product` (full objects) distinction, or keep `Option` to represent 'not loaded'.

## Affected Files (per module)

- `products/domain.rs` — Product fields change from `Option<Uuid>` to `Option<Brand>`, `Option<Category>`
- `brands/domain.rs` — Brand may embed `Vec<Category>` for associated categories
- `categories/domain.rs` — Category may embed `Option<Category>` for parent
- `products/repository.rs` — Extend JOINs to fetch full brand/category data
- `products/dtos.rs` — `TryFrom` construction needs richer input

## Dependencies

- All current domain.rs files must be stable first (brands, categories, products)
- Integration tests for brands (bd-180) and categories (bd-3nl) should pass first

comments.author: JYC11
comments.text: Downgraded to P4 backlog — this is ORM-level scope creep. The current Option<Uuid> approach works fine. Domain objects already have full value-object coverage for their own fields, which is the important part. Traversing FK relationships can stay at the repository/service level via explicit queries.

title: Follow-up: Resilient auth — gRPC to Identity + Redis cache + circuit breaker fallback

comments.author: JYC11
comments.text: Separate plan: Replace claims-based auth with gRPC call to Identity service. Add Redis cache for gRPC results (same pattern as Identity user cache). Add periodic health check (background task, AtomicBool flag) to determine Identity gRPC availability. Circuit breaker pattern: healthy -> gRPC + cache, unhealthy -> fallback to JWT claims. Consider failsafe crate for circuit breaker implementation.

comments.text: Add new_claims_based() constructor to AuthMiddleware in shared/src/auth/middleware.rs. When trust_claims=true, handle() constructs CurrentUser from AccessTokenClaims (sub + role) instead of calling get_by_id(). Backward-compatible — Identity keeps using new(). Also add NoGrpc type alias in shared/src/server.rs.
